description: CT Abdomen and Pelvis with IV contrast
indication: Acute RUQ/epigastric pain, fever, leukocytosis, elevated lipase; concern for acute pancreatitis or biliary obstruction. Patient has documented iodinated-contrast allergy; received emergent oral prednisone 50 mg in the ED prior to contrast administration.
technique: Multidetector CT of the abdomen and pelvis was performed with IV iodinated contrast. 5 mm axial reconstructions with coronal and sagittal reformats. Premedication for contrast allergy documented (prednisone 50 mg PO at 5:30 PM, single dose; diphenhydramine deferred given alertness concerns in the acute setting).
findings:
PANCREAS: Diffuse enlargement of the pancreatic body and tail with surrounding peripancreatic stranding consistent with acute interstitial pancreatitis. No pancreatic necrosis identified on contrast-enhanced phases. No fluid collections meeting criteria for pseudocyst or walled-off necrosis at this time.

GALLBLADDER: Distended. Contains multiple stones (cholelithiasis), the largest measuring 1.4 cm. Gallbladder wall thickness 4 mm (mildly thickened). Trace pericholecystic stranding without organized fluid collection.

BILIARY TREE: Intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation present. Common bile duct measures 9 mm in maximal diameter. A subtle 6 mm filling defect is visualized within the distal CBD, consistent with choledocholithiasis.

LIVER: Normal size and contour. No focal lesion. No biliary obstruction at the hilum.

SPLEEN, ADRENALS, KIDNEYS: Within normal limits. No hydronephrosis. Bilateral renal cortical thinning consistent with known chronic kidney disease.

BOWEL: No evidence of obstruction, perforation, or wall thickening. Ileus of proximal small bowel is suggested.

VASCULATURE: Patent aorta and major branches. No evidence of mesenteric venous thrombosis. No portal vein thrombosis.

LYMPH NODES: Mildly prominent reactive lymph nodes in the porta hepatis and peripancreatic region.

BONES: No acute fracture. Mild degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

LUNG BASES (visible on chest images): No basal infiltrates or effusions on this study. (Detailed chest imaging not performed today.)

FREE FLUID: Trace free fluid in the pelvis and around the pancreas.
impression:
1. Acute interstitial pancreatitis with peripancreatic stranding; no pancreatic necrosis.
2. CHOLELITHIASIS WITH SUSPECTED CHOLEDOCHOLITHIASIS — distended gallbladder with multiple stones and a 6 mm filling defect in the distal common bile duct with associated intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation (CBD 9 mm).
3. Mild gallbladder wall thickening without organized pericholecystic fluid — early acute cholecystitis cannot be excluded.
4. No abdominal abscess at this time.
5. Findings consistent with acute biliary pancreatitis from a passed or impacted CBD stone. Recommend urgent ERCP for diagnostic and therapeutic evaluation.
6. Reactive porta hepatis lymphadenopathy, consistent with the acute inflammatory process.
comparison: No prior abdominal CT available for comparison. Prior abdominal ultrasound 9/30/2025 documented cholelithiasis without acute cholecystitis.
